name: Herzog Zwei
description: Herzog Zwei is a real-time strategy game where two players control transforming mechs and command units to conquer the enemy base. The game takes place on a split-screen battlefield, featuring diverse terrains and strategically important locations like factories and outposts. Players directly control their mech, which can transform between a fast-moving jet and a walking assault unit. As a jet, the mech can quickly traverse the map, transport units, and scout enemy positions. As an assault unit, it is better suited for combat. Players indirectly control various ground and air units which are spawned at factories. These units follow pre-programmed attack and defend routines but their type and destination are determined by the player. The core gameplay loop involves capturing factories to increase unit production speed and overall resource generation. The goal is to push into the enemy territory, destroy the enemy's base, and eliminate their mech. Key to victory is skillful unit deployment and a good understanding of map geography. Different unit types have strengths and weaknesses against other types, and utilizing these effectively is a crucial aspect of the gameplay. The game features a limited amount of resources that are generated over time at the player's base, and the cost of each unit type depends on these resources.

controls.arrow_up: Move mech forward / Scroll map up
controls.arrow_down: Move mech backward / Scroll map down
controls.arrow_left: Turn mech left / Scroll map left
controls.arrow_right: Turn mech right / Scroll map right
controls.q: Issue attack command to selected units
controls.e: Issue defend command to selected units
controls.z: Deploy selected unit type to selected factory/outpost
